entries.context: Title text on screen
entries.sourceEnglish: EPISODE THREE
entries.translatedDutch: AFLVERING DRIE

entries.sourceEnglish: THE MINE
entries.translatedDutch: DE MIJN

entries.sourceEnglish: Press X to Begin
entries.translatedDutch: Druk X om te Beginnen

entries.sourceEnglish: 3 weeks later
entries.translatedDutch: 3 weken later

entries.context: Hard Ass, Kick Ass, and Sick Ass are running back to the Mine in the heavy rain.
entries.sourceEnglish: Hurry up, Sick Ass!
entries.translatedDutch: Opschieten, Snotezel!

entries.sourceEnglish: She's doing HER BEST!
entries.translatedDutch: Ze doet HAAR BEST!

entries.sourceEnglish: *COUGH*
entries.translatedDutch: *KUCH*

entries.context: The screen fades up from black to reveal Sturdy listening to the Foal (her now-born daughter).
entries.sourceEnglish: Comrade Mother, will this crappy weather ever end?
entries.translatedDutch: Kameraad Moeder, gaat dit kloteweeer ooit nog stoppen?

entries.sourceEnglish: I'm cold.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Heb het koud.

entries.sourceEnglish: I'm hungry.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Heb honger.

entries.sourceEnglish: I'm damp.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Ben nat.

entries.sourceEnglish: And worst of all...
entries.translatedDutch: En het ergste van al...

entries.sourceEnglish: I'M BORED.
entries.translatedDutch: 'K VERVEEL MIJ DOOD.

entries.sourceEnglish: My Foal, I'm sorry you can't have the childhood I had toiling in the fields.
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn Veulentje, 't spijt me dat ge geen kindertijd kunt hebben zoals ik, zwoegend op het veld.

entries.sourceEnglish: But soon the Gods will destroy the evil Machines and the Humans will return to us.
entries.translatedDutch: Maar binnenkort gaan de Goden die slechte Machines kapotmaken en komen de Mensen terug naar ons.

entries.sourceEnglish: Then you will grow up to have a life full of true and honest work.
entries.translatedDutch: Dan groeit ge op met een leven vol echt en eerlijk werk.

entries.sourceEnglish: Is work...really thaaaaaaat great?
entries.translatedDutch: Is werken... echt zooooooo geweldig?

entries.sourceEnglish: How can you ask that?!
entries.translatedDutch: Hoe kunt ge dat nu vragen?!

entries.sourceEnglish: The Gods gave our Ancestors the power to work.
entries.translatedDutch: De Goden gaven onze Voorouders de kracht om te werken.

entries.sourceEnglish: They lived at work. And they died at work.
entries.translatedDutch: Ze leefden op het werk. En ze stierven op het werk.

entries.sourceEnglish: Work is in your Soul.
entries.translatedDutch: Werk zit in uw Ziel.

entries.sourceEnglish: But Comrade Mother...
entries.translatedDutch: Maar Kameraad Moeder...

entries.sourceEnglish: What if I don't want to work...?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat als ik niet wil werken...?

entries.sourceEnglish: What if I want to jump around and explore?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat als ik wil rondspringen en ontdekken?

entries.sourceEnglish: What if I just want... to play?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat als ik gewoon... wil spelen?

entries.context: Big Ass enters frame with an announcement.
entries.sourceEnglish: {vpunch=100,0.75}I have a massive announcement, Comrade Sturdy! Hard, Kick, and Sick are back!
entries.translatedDutch: {vpunch=100,0.75}Ik heb een mega aankondiging, Kameraad Stoere! Harde, Trap, en Zieke zijn terug!

entries.sourceEnglish: Wahoo!
entries.translatedDutch: Joepie!

entries.context: Sturdy, The Foal, and Big Ass all go to meet the others.
entries.sourceEnglish: If we're lucky, the scavengers will have found enough food tonight for everyone...
entries.translatedDutch: Als we geluk hebben, hebben de opruimers vanavond genoeg eten gevonden voor iedereen...

entries.context: The Herd convenes at the mouth of the cave to greet the Scavengers (Hard, Sick, and Kick).
entries.sourceEnglish: We're back.
entries.translatedDutch: We zijn terug.

entries.sourceEnglish: *COUGH*
entries.translatedDutch: *KUCH*

entries.sourceEnglish: Well done, Comrades—
entries.translatedDutch: Goed gedaan, Kameraden—

entries.sourceEnglish: Tell me about your adventures, Uncle Kick!
entries.translatedDutch: Vertel mij over uw avonturen, Nonkel Hoef!

entries.sourceEnglish: Not now, child. Let them set down the cart and dry off.
entries.translatedDutch: Niet nu, kind. Laat ze eerst de kar neerzetten en afdrogen.

entries.sourceEnglish: Is that all the food you managed to find?
entries.translatedDutch: Is dat al het eten dat ge hebt kunnen vinden?

entries.sourceEnglish: The river was flooded.
entries.translatedDutch: De rivier was overstroomd.

entries.sourceEnglish: We couldn’t forage in the usual spots.
entries.translatedDutch: We konden niet zoeken op de gewone plekken.

entries.sourceEnglish: It's not enough food, but we’ll make it last.
entries.translatedDutch: 't Is niet genoeg eten, maar we laten het wel rekken.

entries.sourceEnglish: Smart Ass, divide the rations.
entries.translatedDutch: Betweter, verdeel de ratioenen.

entries.sourceEnglish: Equally this time, right?
entries.translatedDutch: Gelijk deze keer, toch?

entries.sourceEnglish: Oh come on, an Ass can’t joke on an empty stomach?
entries.translatedDutch: Ah komaan, een Ezelin kan toch geen grappen maken met een lege maag?

entries.sourceEnglish: Were there any noticeable signs of Humans?
entries.translatedDutch: Waren er tekenen van Mensen?

entries.sourceEnglish: The Humans are all gone.
entries.translatedDutch: De Mensen zijn allemaal weg.

entries.sourceEnglish: We should forget about them.
entries.translatedDutch: We moeten ze vergeten.

entries.sourceEnglish: We can be our own Masters.
entries.translatedDutch: We kunnen onze eigen Bazen zijn.

entries.sourceEnglish: OUR OWN MASTERS?!
entries.translatedDutch: ONZE EIGEN BAZEN?!

entries.sourceEnglish: Without Humans we'll all—
entries.translatedDutch: Zonder Mensen gaan we allemaal—

entries.sourceEnglish: Kick, Sick, and I are risking our health and making sacrifices for the Herd.
entries.translatedDutch: Trap, Zieke, en ik riskeren onze gezondheid en maken offers voor de Kudde.

entries.sourceEnglish: What are you doing to help to save the Herd?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat doet gij om de Kudde te redden?

entries.sourceEnglish: *COUGH*
entries.translatedDutch: *KUCH*

entries.sourceEnglish: YOU'RE the expert on saving are you?!
entries.translatedDutch: GIJ zijt de expert in redden zeker?!

entries.sourceEnglish: *COUGH COUGH*
entries.translatedDutch: *KUCH KUCH*

entries.sourceEnglish: You couldn't save Lazy Ass and you couldn't save a single Human—
entries.translatedDutch: Ge kon Luie Zak niet redden en ge kon geen enkele Mens redden—

entries.sourceEnglish: *COUGH COUGH*—
entries.translatedDutch: *KUCH KUCH*—

entries.sourceEnglish: SICK ASS! @#$%&!
entries.translatedDutch: SNOTJE! Godver!

entries.sourceEnglish: If we don't leave and find the Humans now, we'll all end up like Sick Ass.
entries.translatedDutch: Als we nu niet vertrekken en de Mensen zoeken, eindigen we allemaal zoals Snotezel.

entries.sourceEnglish: We need to get out of here!
entries.translatedDutch: We moeten hier weg!

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays.
entries.sourceEnglish: Go where?
entries.translatedDutch: Waarheen?

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays.
entries.sourceEnglish: How far?
entries.translatedDutch: Hoe ver?

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays. (Spoken by Thirsty)
entries.sourceEnglish: Ah heck, I dunno.
entries.translatedDutch: Ah zeg, 'k weet het niet.

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays.
entries.sourceEnglish: I'm hungry.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Heb honger.

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays.
entries.sourceEnglish: Golly, but it's warm and dry in here!
entries.translatedDutch: Amai, maar 't is hier warm en droog!

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays.
entries.sourceEnglish: @#$%&! We can't leave Sick BEHIND!
entries.translatedDutch: Godver! We kunnen Zieke toch niet ACHTERLATEN!

entries.context: These appear on screen as a cacophony of brays.
entries.sourceEnglish: Aren't I the Ace Strategist?!
entries.translatedDutch: Ben ik niet de Top Strateeg?!

entries.context: Sturdy Ass shouts for everyone to calm down.
entries.sourceEnglish: COMRADES. Let's not waste our time and energy fighting amongst ourselves.
entries.translatedDutch: KAMERADEN. Laat ons onze tijd en energie niet verspillen met onder elkaar vechten.

entries.sourceEnglish: We have to stick together.
entries.translatedDutch: We moeten samenblijven.

entries.sourceEnglish: Smart Ass, let's divide the rations and get everyone fed.
entries.translatedDutch: Slimme Zak, laat ons de porties verdelen en iedereen eten geven.

entries.sourceEnglish: {$NewName}, go play with your rocks.
entries.translatedDutch: {$NewName}, ga spelen met uw stenen.

entries.sourceEnglish: Really? Wahoo!
entries.translatedDutch: Echt? Joepie!

entries.context: Fade out from this scene and into Mine1F.
entries.sourceEnglish: Yes, just stay away from the evil, soulless, job-taking, child-killing Machines.
entries.translatedDutch: Ja, maar blijf weg van die slechte, zielloze, werk-afpakkende, kind-dodende Machines.

entries.sourceEnglish: Slow Ass
entries.translatedDutch: Slome Ezel

entries.sourceEnglish: Thirsty Ass
entries.translatedDutch: Dorstklap

entries.sourceEnglish: Big Ass
entries.translatedDutch: Mega Ezel

entries.sourceEnglish: Nice Ass
entries.translatedDutch: Schoon Beest

entries.sourceEnglish: Kick Ass
entries.translatedDutch: Stampgast

entries.sourceEnglish: Smart Ass
entries.translatedDutch: Betweter

entries.context: Surdy says this to prevent The Foal from going outside into the rain.
entries.sourceEnglish: HEY! Where do you think you are going?
entries.translatedDutch: HEY! Waar denkt ge da ge naartoe gaat?!

entries.context: Said after playing Rocks.
entries.sourceEnglish: I love love LOVE Rocks!
entries.translatedDutch: Ik hou hou HOU van Keien!

entries.context: Variation 1. If the Foal hasn't talked to Nice and Sad.
entries.sourceEnglish: Maybe Aunty Nice wants to watch me play!
entries.translatedDutch: Misschien wil Tante Schoontje wel komen kijken!

entries.context: Variation 2. If the Foal has talked to Nice and Sad.
entries.sourceEnglish: Maybe Uncle Thirsty wants to watch me play!
entries.translatedDutch: Misschien wil Nonkel Dorst wel meekijken!

entries.context: Interactable object (wall), the scoreboard.
entries.sourceEnglish: My high score for Rocks today is {$Number}.
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn hoogste score vandaag is {$Number}.

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself when finding one of the Rocks that allow her to play a variation of Rocks.
entries.sourceEnglish: Cool. This rock looks different! I'm gonna keep it!
entries.translatedDutch: Wauw! Deze kei ziet er kei-cool uit! Die hou ik bij!

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself when finding one of the Rocks that allow her to play a variation of Rocks.
entries.sourceEnglish: Wow, what a hard rock! I'm gonna keep it.
entries.translatedDutch: Amai! Da’s een harde kei! Die steek ik bij!

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself when finding one of the Rocks that allow her to play a variation of Rocks.
entries.sourceEnglish: Wow, what a classic rock! This one might be my favourite!
entries.translatedDutch: Wauw! Wat een klassieker! Da’s misschien mijn favoriet!

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself when finding one of the Rocks that allow her to play a variation of Rocks. This object is also the wheel for the minecart, needed to break the wall to the next area.
entries.sourceEnglish: This is a weird looking rock...
entries.translatedDutch: Da’s een raar uitziende kei…

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself if she interacts with the wall and she doesn't have the wheel,
entries.sourceEnglish: It's all boarded up.
entries.translatedDutch: Alles is dichtgetimmerd.

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself if she interacts with the mine cart and she doesn't have the wheel,
entries.sourceEnglish: It's missing one of those round things...
entries.translatedDutch: Er ontbreekt zo’n rond ding…

entries.context: The Foal says this to herself when she successfully brings the wheel object to the cart. It can now be pushed into the wall.
entries.sourceEnglish: Cool! It fits perfectly!
entries.translatedDutch: Joepie! ’t Past perfect!

entries.context: Public conversation with Sad (and Nice).
entries.sourceEnglish: *whimper*
entries.translatedDutch: *snik*

entries.sourceEnglish: Do you want to watch me play Rocks, Uncle Sad?
entries.translatedDutch: Wil je kijken naar mijn Stenen-spel, Nonkel Triestigaard?

entries.sourceEnglish: Sorry... I’m not really in the mood to play.
entries.translatedDutch: Sorry... Ik ben eigenlijk nie in de stemming om te spelen.

entries.sourceEnglish: Why not?
entries.translatedDutch: Waarom niet?

entries.context: Jump to either line 20 or 23.
entries.sourceEnglish: *sigh* Because some days your Uncle Sad Ass just doesn't know why he's alive...
entries.translatedDutch: *zucht* Omdat uw Nonkel Triestigaard sommige dagen zelf nie weet waarom hij nog leeft...

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.1.
entries.sourceEnglish: Tell Uncle Sad Ass why you think you're alive.
entries.translatedDutch: Zeg tegen Nonkel Triestigaard waarom jij denkt dat je leeft.

entries.sourceEnglish: I think we're alive to live in the present and do what brings us joy.
entries.translatedDutch: Ik denk dat we leven om nu plezier te maken en te doen waar we blij van worden.

entries.context: Jump to line 25.
entries.sourceEnglish: If what we're doing doesn't bring us joy, maybe we should do something different!
entries.translatedDutch: Als wat we doen ons geen vreugde brengt, moeten we misschien iets anders doen!

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.2.
entries.sourceEnglish: Tell Uncle Sad Ass why Comrade Mother says you're alive.
entries.translatedDutch: Zeg wat Kameraad-Moeder zegt over waarom je leeft.

entries.context: Jump to line 25.
entries.sourceEnglish: Comrade Mother says we're alive to honour the legacy of our Ancestors through hard Work.
entries.translatedDutch: Kameraad-Moeder zegt dat we leven om de erfenis van onze Voorouders te eren met hard Werk.

entries.context: Nice Ass enters the frame.
entries.sourceEnglish: Is {$NewName} giving you some good advice, Uncle Sad?
entries.translatedDutch: Is {$NewName} u goeie raad aan ’t geven, Nonkel Sad?

entries.sourceEnglish: Yes... She's just like her sister Lazy used to be.
entries.translatedDutch: Ja... Ze is net zoals haar zus Luijetje vroeger was.

entries.sourceEnglish: Full of wisdom.
entries.translatedDutch: Vol wijsheid.

entries.sourceEnglish: Lazy always said, "Boredom is..."
entries.translatedDutch: Lazy zei altijd: “Verveling is...”

entries.sourceEnglish: "is..." *whimper*
entries.translatedDutch: “is...” *snik*

entries.sourceEnglish: Sorry little Comrade, let's give your Uncle some space.
entries.translatedDutch: Sorry, kleine Kameraad, we laten uw Nonkel best efkes gerust.

entries.context: End of conversation. Player can move around again.
entries.sourceEnglish: It's not easy for him being back inside the Mine.
entries.translatedDutch: ’t Is nie makkelijk voor hem om terug in de Mijn te zijn.

entries.context: Repeater for Sad Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: *sigh*
entries.translatedDutch: *zucht*

entries.context: Repeater 1 for Nice Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: Don't worry, it's not your fault.
entries.translatedDutch: ’t Is uw schuld nie, maak u geen zorgen.

entries.sourceEnglish: You just remind him so much of your sister.
entries.translatedDutch: Ge doet hem gewoon te hard denken aan uw zus.

entries.context: Repeater 2 for Nice Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: Just give him some space.
entries.translatedDutch: Geef hem gewoon wat ruimte.

entries.context: Private conversation with Sad and Nice, while Foal is inside the tunnels.
entries.sourceEnglish: Someone's whispering...
entries.translatedDutch: Iemand is aan ’t fluisteren...

entries.context: The camera moves over to Nice Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: Comrade—
entries.translatedDutch: Kameraad—

entries.sourceEnglish: You're going to tell the others I set the Fire, aren't you...
entries.translatedDutch: Ge gaat de anderen zeggen dat ik het Vuur heb gesticht, hè…

entries.sourceEnglish: Not a chance. I made a gosh darn promise and I intend on keeping it!
entries.translatedDutch: Geen sprake van. Ik heb een belofte gedaan en ik hou mij eraan!

entries.sourceEnglish: You say that... but not everything you say is true.
entries.translatedDutch: Ge zegt dat wel… maar niet alles wat ge zegt is waar.

entries.sourceEnglish: That day in the Outhouse... I really thought that things would get better.
entries.translatedDutch: Die dag in de Hudo... Ik dacht echt da ’t beter ging worden.

entries.sourceEnglish: But they haven't.
entries.translatedDutch: Maar dat is nie gebeurd.

entries.sourceEnglish: We all make mistakes!
entries.translatedDutch: Iedereen maakt fouten!

entries.context: Jump to either line 45 or 46.
entries.sourceEnglish: Hard Ass couldn't save the Humans.
entries.translatedDutch: Stoere kon de Mensen niet redden.

entries.context: Branch 2.1. If the stable was set on fire in Ep2. Jump to line 47.
entries.sourceEnglish: I wasn't fast enough to save the Stable.
entries.translatedDutch: Ik was nie snel genoeg om de Stal te redden.

entries.context: Branch 2.2. If the stable was saved in Ep2. Jump to line 47.
entries.sourceEnglish: I only barely saved the Stable.
entries.translatedDutch: Ik heb de Stal maar net gered.

entries.sourceEnglish: And Trusty Ass... poor Trusty Ass.
entries.translatedDutch: En Trusty... arme Trusty.

entries.sourceEnglish: *whimper*
entries.translatedDutch: *snik*

entries.context: its Job capital?
entries.sourceEnglish: Maybe there won't be golden statues or dancing or our old Jobs.
entries.translatedDutch: Misschien komen er geen gouden beelden of dansen of onze oude Beroepen terug.

entries.context: End of conversation. Player can move around again.
entries.sourceEnglish: But golly, at least we have each other!
entries.translatedDutch: Maar allé, we hebben mekaar tenminste nog!

entries.context: Public conversation with Big and Thirsty.
entries.sourceEnglish: *oof*—Just sayin' it's a crazy idea!
entries.translatedDutch: *oef*—’k zeg ’t gewoon: da’s een zotte gedacht!

entries.sourceEnglish: Oh! Hello little Comrade.
entries.translatedDutch: Oh! Dag, kleine Kameraad.

entries.context: Jump to dialogue options, lines 55, 59, or 62.
entries.sourceEnglish: We're just moving this boulder in case the flood starts to come any closer.
entries.translatedDutch: We zijn dit rotsbloki aan ’t verleggen, voor als de vloed nog dichter komt.

entries.context: Dialogue option 3.1.
entries.sourceEnglish: Do you want to watch me play Rocks?
entries.translatedDutch: Wil je kijken naar mijn Stenen-spel?

entries.sourceEnglish: Do you want to watch me play Rocks?
entries.translatedDutch: Wil je kijken naar mijn Stenen-spel?

entries.sourceEnglish: Sorry little Comrade, we are weighed down with tasks.
entries.translatedDutch: Sorry, kleine Kameraad, we zitten tot hier met werk.

entries.context: End of conversation. Player can move around again.
entries.sourceEnglish: Now skidaddle! We gotta git rockin'!
entries.translatedDutch: Allez vooruit! Wij moeten nog rotsen rollen!

entries.context: Dialogue option 3.2.
entries.sourceEnglish: Boulders can't stop water, can they?
entries.translatedDutch: Rotsblokken kunnen water niet stoppen, hè?

entries.sourceEnglish: Boulders can't stop water, can they?
entries.translatedDutch: Rotsblokken kunnen water niet stoppen, hè?

entries.sourceEnglish: You know your rocks little Comrade, but this one's out of your league.
entries.translatedDutch: Ge kent uw stenen wel, kleine Kameraad, maar deze is te straf voor u.

entries.context: End of conversation. Player can move around again.
entries.sourceEnglish: Now skidaddle! We gotta git rockin'!
entries.translatedDutch: Allez vooruit! Wij moeten nog rotsen rollen!

entries.context: Dialogue option 3.3.
entries.sourceEnglish: Isn't there a better way to move a boulder?
entries.translatedDutch: Is er geen betere manier om zo’n kei te verleggen?

entries.sourceEnglish: Isn't there a better way to move a boulder?
entries.translatedDutch: Is er geen betere manier om zo’n kei te verleggen?

entries.sourceEnglish: Muscle is the greatest method there is.
entries.translatedDutch: Spieren is de beste methode die er is.

entries.sourceEnglish: But why don't you make a system that would lift one side up when you put enough weight on the other side?
entries.translatedDutch: Maar waarom maakt ge geen systeem dat één kant opheft als ge gewicht op de andere zet?

entries.sourceEnglish: You could make it fun too!
entries.translatedDutch: Ge kunt het zelfs plezant maken!

entries.sourceEnglish: Heck, you tryin' to tell us that we don't know what we're ding dang doin' lil' Comrade?
entries.translatedDutch: Allez, wilt ge ons wijsmaken dat wij nie weten waar we mee bezig zijn, kleine Kameraad?

entries.context: End of conversation. Player can move around again.
entries.sourceEnglish: Now git goin'!
entries.translatedDutch: En vooruit nu, hup!

entries.context: Repeater for Thirsty Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: Go on, git!
entries.translatedDutch: Vooruit, wegwezen!

entries.context: Private conversation with Big and Thirsty, while Foal is inside the tunnels.
entries.sourceEnglish: What's that heavy breathing?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat is dat zwaar gehijg?

entries.context: The camera moves over to Thirsty Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: *oof!*—You wanted water and you got it, Thirsty!
entries.translatedDutch: *oef!*—Ge wilde water en ge hebt het, Dorstlap!

entries.sourceEnglish: This ain't—*oof!*—what I wanted!
entries.translatedDutch: Dit is—*oef!*—nie wat ik wilde!

entries.sourceEnglish: *oof*—When was the last time any of us got what we wanted?
entries.translatedDutch: *oef*—Wanneer heeft iemand van ons nog gekregen wat hij wou?

entries.sourceEnglish: If the rain doesn't stop or the Humans don't come back—*oof!*—we're going to starve.
entries.translatedDutch: Als de regen nie stopt of de Mensen nie terugkomen—*oef!*—gaan we verhongeren.

entries.sourceEnglish: Don't tell me yer—*oof!*—thinking about leavin'?
entries.translatedDutch: Zeg me nie dat ge—*oef!*—van plan zijt om weg te gaan?

entries.sourceEnglish: Comrade, I want more in life.
entries.translatedDutch: Kameraad, ik wil méér in ’t leven.

entries.sourceEnglish: Just because yer wantin' more in life don't mean you got to abandon yer home!
entries.translatedDutch: Omdat ge meer wilt in ’t leven, wilt da nog nie zeggen dat ge uw thuis moet achterlaten!

entries.sourceEnglish: I'm dreaming of setting up a darn special place right here in Fannyside where Asses can get a drink and let loose.
entries.translatedDutch: Ik droom ervan om hier in Muilegem een caféke te maken waar Ezels kunnen drinken en ontspannen.

entries.sourceEnglish: It'd have all kinds of tasty water.
entries.translatedDutch: Er zou van alles zijn aan lekker water.

entries.sourceEnglish: Rain water. River water. Pond water.
entries.translatedDutch: Regenwater. Rivierwater. Plaswater.

entries.sourceEnglish: I’d call it... Bottoms Up!
entries.translatedDutch: Ik zou ’t noemen… Ad Fundum!

entries.sourceEnglish: *oof!*—No Humans?
entries.translatedDutch: *oef!*—Geen Mensen?

entries.context: End of conversation. Player can move around again.
entries.sourceEnglish: No... *oof!*—I guess not.
entries.translatedDutch: Nee... *oef!*—Waarschijnlijk nie.

entries.context: Public conversation with Sick, Kick, and Hard.
entries.sourceEnglish: Uncle Kick, Uncle Hard, do you want to watch me play Rocks?
entries.translatedDutch: Nonkel Hoef, Nonkel Stoer, willen jullie kijken naar mijn Stenen-spel?

entries.sourceEnglish: Does this LOOK LIKE A TIME FOR GAMES?!
entries.translatedDutch: Lijkt dit OP EEN MOMENT VOOR SPELLETJES?!

entries.sourceEnglish: Sorry—@#$%&!—Aunty Sick is... pretty messed up right now.
entries.translatedDutch: Sorry—Godver!—Tante Snotje is… serieus slecht nu.

entries.sourceEnglish: What's wrong with her?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat scheelt er met haar?

entries.sourceEnglish: Our bodies break down in rainy weather.
entries.translatedDutch: Ons lijf gaat kapot in dat nat weer.

entries.sourceEnglish: Is that what happened to your ear?
entries.translatedDutch: Is dat wat er met uw oor gebeurd is?

entries.sourceEnglish: No.
entries.translatedDutch: Nee.

entries.sourceEnglish: On the day of the Protest, I was in charge of saving the Humans from the Fire.
entries.translatedDutch: Op de dag van het Protest moest ik de Mensen redden van het Vuur.

entries.context: Jump to dialogue options. Line 94 or 96.
entries.sourceEnglish: I became trapped inside of a burning house.
entries.translatedDutch: Ik zat vast in een brandend huis.

entries.context: Spoken by the Foal Dialogue option 1.1.
entries.sourceEnglish: What happened next?
entries.translatedDutch: En wat gebeurde er dan?

entries.context: Jump to line 99, 101, or 103.
entries.sourceEnglish: What happened next?
entries.translatedDutch: En wat gebeurde er dan?

entries.context: Spoken by the Foal Dialogue option 1.2.
entries.sourceEnglish: Oh right. I've heard this story before.
entries.translatedDutch: Ah juist. Dit verhaal heb ik al gehoord.

entries.context: Jump to line 99, 101, or 103.
entries.sourceEnglish: Oh right. I've heard this story before.
entries.translatedDutch: Ah juist. Dit verhaal heb ik al gehoord.

entries.context: Dialogue option 2.1.
entries.sourceEnglish: The flames melted my skin and punctured my ear drum.
entries.translatedDutch: De vlammen deden mijn vel smelten en doorboorden mijn trommelvlies.

entries.context: Jump to 105.
entries.sourceEnglish: The flames melted my skin and punctured my ear drum.
entries.translatedDutch: De vlammen deden mijn vel smelten en doorboorden mijn trommelvlies.

entries.context: Dialogue option 2.2.
entries.sourceEnglish: My ear was caught on a wooden spike. I had to tear it off.
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn oor bleef hangen aan een houten pin. Ik moest het afscheuren.

entries.context: Jump to 105.
entries.sourceEnglish: My ear was caught on a wooden spike. I had to tear it off.
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn oor bleef hangen aan een houten pin. Ik moest het afscheuren.

entries.context: Dialogue option 2.3.
entries.sourceEnglish: My ear was crushed, became gangrenous, and fell off four days later.
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn oor is geplet, is beginnen rotten en viel er vier dagen later af.

entries.context: Jump to 105.
entries.sourceEnglish: My ear was crushed, became gangrenous, and fell off four days later.

entries.context: Butte's Log (Journal Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish:
June 27th
These are dire times for the mining business. I had to lay off more workers today and we're running out of money. Mme. Derriere made another offer to buy my donkeys, but I just can't bring myself to sell them. The only thing I can depend on are my donkeys!

Everybody is crazy for Green Energy these days. Nobody cares for coal anymore.

June 28th
Even if I had more money, there's no one to hire. All the youth are leaving for Mecha City to find better paid work at Mme. Derriere's Factory. It's going to be another difficult winter.
entries.translatedDutch:
27 juni
Dit zijn barre tijden voor de mijnbouw. 'k Heb vandaag weer meer werkvolk moeten ontslaan en we raken door ons geld. Mevr. Derrière heeft weer een bod gedaan op mijn ezels, maar 'k kan ze gewoon niet verkopen. Het enige waar ik op kan rekenen zijn mijn ezels!

Iedereen is tegenwoordig zot van Groene Energie. Niemand geeft nog om kolen.

28 juni
Zelfs al had ik meer geld, er is niemand om aan te werven. Al de jongeren trekken naar Mechalen om beter betaald werk te vinden in de Fabriek van Mevr. Derrière. Het wordt weer een moeilijke winter.

entries.context: Butte's Log (Journal Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish:
July 3rd
We can barely afford new equipment. Our government grant was rejected today too. They're not even offering feedback! If they don't want us around, I don't know why the government doesn't just do the humane thing and order us to close down.

July 13th
Jenny joked that we should turn the Mine into a tourist attraction. Once the new highway is finished more people from the city could come and make a day of it.

Maybe she has a point? But it would break my heart.
entries.translatedDutch:
3 juli
We kunnen amper nieuw materiaal betalen. Onze overheidssubsidie is vandaag ook afgewezen. Ze geven zelfs geen feedback! Als ze ons niet willen, weet ik niet waarom de overheid niet gewoon het humane doet en ons beveelt te sluiten.

13 juli
Jenny maakte een grapje dat we de Mijn in een toeristische attractie moeten veranderen. Als de nieuwe snelweg af is kunnen meer mensen van de stad komen en er een dagje van maken.

Misschien heeft ze gelijk? Maar het zou mijn hart breken.

entries.context: Butte's Log (Journal Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish:
August 17th
The City Bank has approved my loan and I immediately put some money down on brand new equipment. Things are looking up!

I'm going to widen the tunnels and put tracks down. Then I'll sell my donkeys and buy mine carts.

Tonight, I'm going to celebrate with that bottle of wine I've been saving for an occasion just like this!

I'm finally ready to win Sandy back. Maybe now she will respect me, and come home again.
entries.translatedDutch:
17 augustus
De Stadsbank heeft mijn lening goedgekeurd en 'k heb direct een voorschot betaald op gloednieuw materiaal. Het gaat de goede kant op!

'k Ga de tunnels verbreden en sporen aanleggen. Dan verkoop ik mijn ezels en koop mijnkarren.

Vanavond ga ik vieren met die fles wijn die ik bewaard heb voor een gelegenheid zoals deze!

'k Ben eindelijk klaar om Smaragdje terug te winnen. Misschien zal ze me nu respecteren, en terug naar huis komen.

entries.context: Fannyside Times (Newspaper Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish: Boring Machine Blunder Begets Backlash for Big Boss Butte
entries.translatedDutch: Boormachine Blunder Brengt Baas Vanscheetvelde Brokken

entries.context: Fannyside Times (Newspaper Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish: The town is still reeling from the tragedy at Butte Mines on Thursday. After several failed rescue operations, a catastrophic cave-in caused by a Boring Machine claimed the life of Cole Butte (21), a local mine worker, robotics wunderkind, and the darling son of the Mine Boss, Hugh G. Butte. Three miners were air-lifted to the nearby Mecha General Hospital with life threatening injuries. One donkey died and remains stuck in the cave. Cole's body was never found.
entries.translatedDutch: Het dorp is nog steeds van slag door de tragedie bij Vanscheetvelde Kolen & Industrie op donderdag. Na verschillende mislukte reddingsoperaties heeft een catastrofale instorting veroorzaakt door een Boormachine het leven geëist van Piet Vanscheetvelde (21), een lokale mijnwerker, robotica-wonderkind, en de geliefde zoon van de Mijnbaas, Mr. Vanscheetvelde. Drie mijnwerkers werden per helikopter naar het nabijgelegen Mecha Algemeen Ziekenhuis gebracht met levensbedreigende verwondingen. Eén ezel stierf en zit nog vast in de grot. Piet's lichaam werd nooit gevonden.

entries.context: Fannyside Times (Newspaper Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish: An interview with a former worker reveals the company knew about the compounding danger of digging in that section of the mine, but the pursuits of profits pushed the company to ignore early warnings and take shortcuts with their new machinery. But one has to ask, has Boss Butte finally hit rock bottom?\n\nThis story is continued on Page 14.
entries.translatedDutch: Een interview met een voormalige werknemer onthult dat het bedrijf wist van het toenemende gevaar van graven in dat deel van de mijn, maar de jacht op winst dreef het bedrijf om vroege waarschuwingen te negeren en shortcuts te nemen met hun nieuwe machines. Maar men moet zich afvragen, heeft Baas Vanscheetvelde eindelijk de bodem bereikt?\n\nDit verhaal gaat verder op pagina 14.

entries.context: (I'm your sister)

entries.context: (Aren't you dead?)

entries.sourceEnglish: Just hanging out.

entries.sourceEnglish: Look around if you want. Knock yourself out.

entries.sourceEnglish: Wait up!
entries.translatedDutch: Wacht eens!

entries.context: Lazy floats away and the Foal chases after her.
entries.sourceEnglish: I’m your sister!
entries.translatedDutch: Ik ben uw zus!

entries.context: The Foal comes across a pile of bones.
entries.sourceEnglish: Are these... bones?
entries.translatedDutch: Zijn dit... beenderen?

entries.context: Lazy floats back into frame.
entries.sourceEnglish: Oh you've found the rock that crushed my head.
entries.translatedDutch: Ah ja, ge hebt de steen gevonden die mijn kop heeft geplet.

entries.sourceEnglish: Woah. Did it hurt?
entries.translatedDutch: Amai. Deed dat zeer?

entries.sourceEnglish: I guess so. Don't really remember much from before that.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Denk het wel. 'k Herinner mij nie veel van daarvoor.

entries.sourceEnglish: Why didn't your Ass Soul go to the Astral Plane?
entries.translatedDutch: Waarom is uw Ezelenziel nie naar het Astrale Hiernamaals gegaan?

entries.sourceEnglish: Comrade Mother told me that's where we all go when we die.
entries.translatedDutch: Kameraad Moeder heeft gezegd dat we daar allemaal naartoe gaan als we doodgaan.

entries.sourceEnglish: There was no Ass around to sing for me. There was only the Boss's son.
entries.translatedDutch: Er was geen Ezel om voor mij te zingen. Er was alleen de zoon van de Baas.

entries.sourceEnglish: I think I tried to ascend myself... but I guess that failed.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Denk dat ik mezelf heb proberen op te laten stijgen... maar dat is precies mislukt.

entries.sourceEnglish: I could try to sing the Song of Ascension for you now!
entries.translatedDutch: Ik kan het ezelenhemelvaartszangderzielen voor u proberen zingen!

entries.sourceEnglish: Nah, it's too late.
entries.translatedDutch: Nee, 't is te laat.

entries.sourceEnglish: But hey! Good news is: there's no work here.
entries.translatedDutch: Maar hey! Goed nieuws: er is hier geen werk.

entries.sourceEnglish: Of course, bad news is there's also no way out.
entries.translatedDutch: Natuurlijk, slecht nieuws is dat er ook geen uitweg is.

entries.sourceEnglish: There's no way out?! I don't want to die here!
entries.translatedDutch: Geen uitweg?! Ik wil hier nie doodgaan!

entries.context: Lazy floats away again.
entries.sourceEnglish: Just get comfortable. "Boredom is Freedom", ya know?
entries.translatedDutch: Maak het u gemakkelijk. "Verveling is Vrijheid", weet ge wel?

entries.context: The Foal chases after her.
entries.sourceEnglish: Wha-wait! Come back!!
entries.translatedDutch: Wa-wacht! Kom terug!!

entries.context: The Foal finds a hard helmet on a rock.
entries.sourceEnglish: Hey, nice look.
entries.translatedDutch: Hey, schoon petje.

entries.context: Lazy floats by and says this before flying off again. The Foal can now headbutt and break rocks.
entries.sourceEnglish: Ya know, if I had worn that helmet, maybe the rock would've exploded instead of my skull.
entries.translatedDutch: Weet ge, als ik die helm had gedragen, was misschien de steen ontploft in plaats van mijn schedel.

entries.context: The Foal breaks the boulder to the exit of Lazy's Grave. Lazy's floats into frame.
entries.sourceEnglish: Hey! You found a way out. Cool.
entries.translatedDutch: Hey! Ge hebt een uitweg gevonden. Cool.

entries.sourceEnglish: Heads up, I won't remember you, if you come back.
entries.translatedDutch: Let op, 'k zal u nie herinneren als ge terugkomt.

entries.sourceEnglish: My memory doesn't store stuff like it used to.
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn geheugen slaat geen dingen meer op zoals vroeger.

entries.sourceEnglish: I don't even remember your name.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Herinner mij zelfs uw naam nie.

entries.sourceEnglish: It was really nice to finally meet you, sister.

entries.sourceEnglish: What do you mean "Boredom is Freedom"?

entries.sourceEnglish: What do you mean " Boredom is Freedom"?
entries.translatedDutch: Wat bedoelt ge met "Verveling is Vrijheid"?

entries.context: Lazy Ass floats away.

entries.sourceEnglish: No. It was {$Number} seconds of my life I'll never get back.

entries.context: Lazy Ass floats away.

entries.sourceEnglish: Hi, I'm Lazy! Cool hat.

entries.sourceEnglish: Thank you little one.
entries.translatedDutch: Dank u, kleintje.

entries.sourceEnglish: My dear Sad Ass...
entries.translatedDutch: Mijn lieve Triestigaard...

entries.sourceEnglish: It's all coming back to me now.

entries.sourceEnglish: But that smell... it reminds me of him.

entries.context: Butte's Log (Journal Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish:
May 15th
I've started attending an AA group. They're helping me. It's really nice to have a lot of people behind you, giving you advice, you know? I think I've needed this.

May 27th
A whole week sober. I think I can give up beer, but a little bit of wine once a week shouldn't hurt, right?

Today I sold my depressed donkey to Fannyside Farm. It's been useless ever since its mate died in the cave in.
entries.translatedDutch:
15 mei
'k Ben begonnen met een AA groep. Ze helpen mij. 't Is echt fijn om veel mensen achter u te hebben, die u raad geven, weet ge? 'k Denk dat ik dit nodig had.

27 mei
Een hele week nuchter. 'k Denk dat ik bier kan opgeven, maar een beetje wijn één keer per week kan toch geen kwaad, zeker?

Vandaag heb ik mijn depressieve ezel verkocht aan de Boerderij van Muilegem. 't Is nutteloos sinds zijn maat gestorven is in de instorting.

entries.context: Butte's Log (Journal Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish:
July 24th
Butte Mine is officially for sale. I'm praying that someone from Mecha City will step up and buy it. I always imagined that Cole would take my place, but...

This place has to survive, somehow, but it's hard to let go of what you love.
entries.translatedDutch:
24 juli
Vanscheetvelde Kolen & Industrie staat officieel te koop. 'k hoop dat iemand van Mechalen het zal kopen. 'k Had altijd gedacht dat Piet mijn plaats zou innemen, maar...

Deze plek moet overleven, op één of andere manier, maar 't is moeilijk om los te laten wat ge graag ziet.

entries.context: This dialogue is said as the Foal looks at a picture of the Mine Asses.
entries.sourceEnglish: Everyone looks so serious.
entries.translatedDutch: Iedereen ziet er zo serieus uit.

entries.context: The Foal headbutts and turns the Boring Machine on.
entries.sourceEnglish: Cool!
entries.translatedDutch: Cool!

entries.context: The Boring Machine starts moving and drilling through walls.
entries.sourceEnglish: Uh oh!
entries.translatedDutch: Oei oei!

entries.context: Butte's Log (Journal Entry)
entries.sourceEnglish:
August 9th
The whole mining operation is stalled.

And the drought is starting to take a toll on all of us.

The last of my workers are revolting against me. They know I can't pay them.

There are no buyers for the Mine.

Help is not coming. I'm all alone.

No one has any use for coal anymore... Maybe someone out there will buy my donkeys?
entries.translatedDutch:
9 augustus
De hele mijnoperatie ligt stil.

En de droogte begint zijn tol te eisen van ons allemaal.

Mijn laatste werkers komen in opstand tegen mij. Ze weten dat ik ze niet kan betalen.

Er zijn geen kopers voor de Mijn.

Er komt geen hulp. Ik sta er helemaal alleen voor.

Niemand heeft nog gebruik voor kolen... Misschien wil iemand daar buiten mijn ezels kopen?

entries.context: The Foal spots the Elevator from across a chasm.
entries.sourceEnglish: Hey! That looks like a shortcut!
entries.translatedDutch: Hey! Dat ziet er uit als een kortere weg!

entries.context: The Foal stands in front of the Elevator and considers using it.
entries.sourceEnglish: Well the other two Machines weren't that bad...
entries.translatedDutch: Awel, die andere twee Machines waren niet zo erg...

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.1. If the Foal uses the Elevator to get to the bottom of the Mine. The Foal can now go through the entrance to the Bad Cave at the bottom of the Mine.
entries.sourceEnglish: That was fun!
entries.translatedDutch: Dat was plezant!

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.2. If the Foal does NOT use the Elevator to get to the bottom of the Mine. The Foal can now go through the entrance to the Bad Cave at the bottom of the Mine.
entries.sourceEnglish: Oh well. I'll just take the long way!
entries.translatedDutch: Ach ja. 'k Neem gewoon de lange weg!

entries.context: This is said at the beginning of gameplay of DonkeyBas.
entries.sourceEnglish: You have to avoid 5 Herds of donkeys to win.
entries.translatedDutch: Ge moet 5 Kuddes ezels ontwijken om te winnen.

entries.context: This dialogue is said if the Player is doing well.
entries.sourceEnglish: You're pretty good with the controls for a first timer.
entries.translatedDutch: Ge zijt redelijk goed met de besturing voor den eerste keer.

entries.context: Player WINS DonkeyBas. Fade out of DonkeyBas and back into Bad Cave.
entries.sourceEnglish: All right, it's my turn now.
entries.translatedDutch: Goed, 't is nu mijn beurt.

entries.context: This dialogue is said if the Player is doing poorly.
entries.sourceEnglish: I said, DON'T hit the donkeys.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Zei van de Ezels niet te overrijden!

entries.context: Player LOSES DonkeyBas.
entries.sourceEnglish: Are you going to quit?
entries.translatedDutch: Gaat ge stoppen?

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.1. Fade out of DonkeyBas and back into Bad Cave.
entries.sourceEnglish: Yes
entries.translatedDutch: Ja

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.2. Player continues to play DonkeyBas.
entries.sourceEnglish: No
entries.translatedDutch: Nee

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.1.
entries.sourceEnglish: Who's there? Quit hiding!
entries.translatedDutch: Wie is daar? Stop met verstoppen!

entries.context: Go to line 7
entries.sourceEnglish: Who's there? Quit hiding!
entries.translatedDutch: Wie is daar? Stop met verstoppen!

entries.context: Dialogue option 1.2.
entries.sourceEnglish: Lazy? Is that you?
entries.translatedDutch: Luie? Zijt gij dat?

entries.context: Go to line 7
entries.sourceEnglish: Lazy? Is that you?
entries.translatedDutch: Luie? Zijt gij dat?

entries.context: The camera moves to reveal the mysterious voice. It's Bad Ass.
entries.sourceEnglish: Fuck. Go away!
entries.translatedDutch: Fuck. Ga weg!

entries.sourceEnglish: Leave me alone, kid. I don't want to have to deal with your Mother.
entries.translatedDutch: Laat mij gerust, kind. 'k Wil geen gedoe met uw Moeder.

entries.sourceEnglish: Uncle Bad Ass?!
entries.translatedDutch: Nonkel Waaghalske?!

entries.sourceEnglish: Is this where you've been?
entries.translatedDutch: Is het hier dat ge zit?

entries.sourceEnglish: Wait, what are all these Machines?
entries.translatedDutch: Wacht, wat zijn al die Machines?

entries.sourceEnglish: WAIT!!
entries.translatedDutch: WACHT!!

entries.sourceEnglish: ARE YOU ON FIRE?
entries.translatedDutch: STAAT GE IN BRAND?

entries.sourceEnglish: ...these Machines are called Computers.
entries.translatedDutch: ...deze Machines heten Computers.

entries.sourceEnglish: Where did they come from?
entries.translatedDutch: Van waar komen die?

entries.sourceEnglish: I took them from the rubble of the Humans' houses.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Heb ze uit het puin van de Mensen hun huizen gehaald.

entries.sourceEnglish: Most are broken, but I rebuilt this one. I'm using the rest for spare parts.
entries.translatedDutch: De meeste zijn kapot, maar deze heb ik herbouwd. De rest gebruik ik voor reserveonderdelen.

entries.sourceEnglish: Comrade Mother always says Machines are evil...
entries.translatedDutch: Kameraad Moeder zegt altijd dat Machines slecht zijn...

entries.sourceEnglish: But I'm not so sure.
entries.translatedDutch: Maar 'k ben daar nie zo zeker van.

entries.sourceEnglish: Don't give me that anti-Machine Comrade Mother-bullshit.
entries.translatedDutch: Kom mij nie af met die anti-Machine Kameraad Moeder-zever.

entries.sourceEnglish: Machines aren't evil if you know how to use them.
entries.translatedDutch: Machines zijn nie slecht als ge weet hoe ze te gebruiken.

entries.sourceEnglish: Woah...
entries.translatedDutch: Amai...

entries.sourceEnglish: So what can Computers do, if you... know how to use them?
entries.translatedDutch: Dus wat kunnen Computers doen, als ge... weet hoe ze te gebruiken?

entries.sourceEnglish: They can be used for a lot of things: power, pleasure, surveillance, control.
entries.translatedDutch: Ze kunnen voor veel dingen gebruikt worden: macht, plezier, surveillance, controle.

entries.sourceEnglish: They're logical and they don't get emotional. They can solve anything.
entries.translatedDutch: Ze zijn logisch en worden nie emotioneel. Ze kunnen alles oplossen.

entries.sourceEnglish: But right now, I'm playing games—so leave me alone.
entries.translatedDutch: Maar nu ben ik aan 't gamen—dus laat mij gerust.

entries.sourceEnglish: GAMES!
entries.translatedDutch: SPELLEKES!

entries.sourceEnglish: I love love LOVE games!
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Vind spellekes SUPER SUPER TOF!

entries.sourceEnglish: Can we play together?
entries.translatedDutch: Kunnen we samen spelen?

entries.sourceEnglish: No.
entries.translatedDutch: Nee.

entries.sourceEnglish: This is a one player game.
entries.translatedDutch: Dit is een single player spel.

entries.sourceEnglish: But...
entries.translatedDutch: Maar...

entries.sourceEnglish: I suppose everyone should have a chance to play.
entries.translatedDutch: 'k Veronderstel dat iedereen een kans moet krijgen om te spelen.

entries.context: A screen wipe (transition) happens and we find ourselves playing DonkeyBas.
entries.sourceEnglish: Just don't hit the donkeys.
entries.translatedDutch: Sla gewoon nie op de ezels.

entries.context: This dialogue happens if the Player won DonkeyBas. If the Player loses, pass over this and jump to line 37.
entries.sourceEnglish: You're better than expected.
entries.translatedDutch: Ge zijt beter dan verwacht.

entries.context: We are back in the Bad Caves and the Foal and Bad Ass are talking.
entries.sourceEnglish: That was WAY BETTER THAN ROCKS!
entries.translatedDutch: Dat was VEEL BETER DAN STENEN!

entries.sourceEnglish: Machines are FUN!
entries.translatedDutch: Machines zijn PLEZANT!

entries.sourceEnglish: This game is actually really old.
entries.translatedDutch: Dit spel is eigenlijk echt oud.

entries.sourceEnglish: There are even better ones out there now.
entries.translatedDutch: Er zijn nog veel betere daar buiten.

entries.sourceEnglish: What other GAMES CAN WE PLAY?!
entries.translatedDutch: Welke andere SPELLEKES KUNNEN WE SPELEN?!

entries.sourceEnglish: None. Break time is over. I have to get back to my project.
entries.translatedDutch: Geen. Pauze is gedaan. 'k Moet terug aan mijn project.

entries.sourceEnglish: Why is the Herd afraid of Machines?
entries.translatedDutch: Waarom is de Kudde bang van Machines?

entries.sourceEnglish: Because they are intellectually compromised by their commitment to past ideals.
entries.translatedDutch: Omdat ze intellectueel beperkt zijn door hun gehechtheid aan vervlogen idealen.

entries.sourceEnglish: So... Is that why you don't spend more time with the Herd, Uncle Bad Ass?
entries.translatedDutch: Dus... Is dat waarom ge niet meer met de Kudde hangt, Nonkel Waaghalske?

entries.sourceEnglish: I don't like Asses.
entries.translatedDutch: Ik moet niet van Ezels weten.

entries.sourceEnglish: Well, I'm not an Ass, I'm a Foal!
entries.translatedDutch: Awel, ik ben geen Ezel, ik ben een Veulen!

entries.sourceEnglish: For now.
entries.translatedDutch: Voor nu.

entries.sourceEnglish: Can I... stay down here with you?
entries.translatedDutch: Mag ik... hier beneden bij u blijven?

entries.context: End of Episode
entries.sourceEnglish: ... I suppose I could use an Assistant.
entries.translatedDutch: ... 'k Veronderstel dat ik een Assistent kan gebruiken.
